summary.goal: Define and implement the core rendering pipeline using TextKit 2 + NSTextView as the performance-critical path from file read to screen render
summary.description:
The rendering pipeline is the core performance-critical path. Every design decision flows from this choice.

## Confirmed: TextKit 2 + NSTextView

macOS 2025+ target confirmed — TextKit 2 is fully available (introduced macOS 12, mature by macOS 14+). No fallback to TextKit 1 needed.

- **NSTextLayoutManager** handles lazy line layout — only lays out lines visible in the viewport
- **NSTextContentStorage** can be fed incrementally as markdown is parsed
- **Native scroll virtualization** — no custom virtual scroll implementation needed
- **Attributed strings** for styled markdown output (headings, bold, code, etc.)
- **Hardware-accelerated** text rendering via Core Text under the hood

## Rejected: WKWebView as Primary

WKWebView adds ~50-100ms overhead for WebKit process startup. Not viable for sub-200ms launch target. Reserved only for isolated complex blocks (math/mermaid) if needed post-v1.

## Syntax Highlighting

Options for Swift:
- **TreeSitter** (C with Swift bindings) — best performance, incremental parsing
- **Splash** (pure Swift) — simpler but less language coverage
- **Highlightr** (wraps highlight.js) — broadest language support but requires JS bridge

Recommendation: TreeSitter for performance-critical path. Incremental parsing means re-highlighting on theme change is near-instant.

## Pipeline Stages

1. **File Read** (< 5ms typical): mmap() or streamed read for large files
2. **Markdown Parse** (background thread): cmark-gfm in C, bound to Swift — parses ~1MB in ~10ms
3. **AST → AttributedString** (progressive): Convert parsed nodes to styled attributed strings in chunks
4. **Syntax Highlight** (per code block): TreeSitter parses code blocks, applies theme colors
5. **Layout** (lazy, TextKit 2): Only compute layout for visible viewport + small buffer
6. **Render** (GPU-accelerated): Core Text renders glyphs, compositor displays

## Memory Budget

- Small files (< 50KB): ~5MB total app memory
- Medium files (50-500KB): ~15-30MB
- Large files (500KB-5MB): ~50-80MB
- Very large (5MB+): ~80-120MB hard cap

## Key Performance Invariants

- Main thread must never block on file I/O or parsing
- First screenful renders before full file is parsed
- Scroll performance: 60fps minimum
- Memory grows sub-linearly with file size
- CPU drops to near-zero when idle

acceptance_criteria.description: Uses NSTextLayoutManager and NSTextContentStorage for lazy layout
Implements TreeSitter, Splash, or Highlightr for code syntax highlighting
File read → Parse → Style → Layout → Render pipeline functions correctly
Stays within defined memory budgets for different file sizes
Main thread never blocks on file I/O or parsing

observations: TextKit 2 is mature on macOS 2025+ target — no fallback needed
NSTextLayoutManager provides native lazy layout — only visible lines computed
NSTextContentStorage supports incremental feeding as markdown is parsed
WKWebView rejected due to 50-100ms startup overhead breaking sub-200ms target
TreeSitter recommended for syntax highlighting — incremental parsing, best performance
cmark-gfm parses ~1MB in ~10ms — fast enough for progressive rendering
Pipeline is 6-stage: File Read → Parse → Style → Highlight → Layout → Render
concerns: Mermaid diagrams require JS runtime — architecture risk for post-v1
Math notation (LaTeX/KaTeX) may need WKWebView fallback for complex rendering

observations: Main thread must never block on I/O or parsing

rejected_alternatives.WKWebView: 50-100ms startup overhead, breaks sub-200ms launch target

steps.title: Define pipeline protocol and data flow types
steps.description: Create the foundational protocol types that define the rendering pipeline's data flow. Define: (1) MarkdownDocument — holds file URL, raw content reference, and parsed AST; (2) RenderChunk — a segment of styled NSAttributedString ready for TextKit 2; (3) PipelineStage protocol — input/output contract for each stage; (4) PipelineCoordinator — orchestrates stages, manages lifecycle. Place these in ViewMD/Engine/Pipeline/. These types are the contract that all pipeline stages implement.

acceptance_criteria.description: MarkdownDocument type holds file URL, content reference, and cmark AST node pointer
RenderChunk type wraps NSAttributedString with source range and metadata
PipelineStage protocol defines process(input:) -> output with async support
PipelineCoordinator can chain stages and manages cancellation

architecture.concurrency: Swift Concurrency (async/await) with structured task groups

steps.title: Implement file reading stage with streaming support
steps.description: Implement the first pipeline stage: FileReader. For small files (< 50KB), read entire contents into memory as String. For medium files (50KB-500KB), read in 10KB chunks. For large files (500KB+), use memory-mapped I/O (mmap via Data(contentsOf:options:.mappedIfSafe)). The stage must run on a background queue (QOS: .userInitiated) and never touch the main thread. Return a MarkdownDocument with the content reference.

acceptance_criteria.description: Files < 50KB read in single pass in under 5ms
Files > 500KB use memory-mapped I/O
All file reading occurs on background queue, never main thread
Returns MarkdownDocument with content ready for parsing

steps.title: Implement markdown parsing stage with cmark-gfm
steps.description: Implement the MarkdownParser pipeline stage. Takes file content and produces a cmark AST. Use cmark_parser_new_with_extensions() with GFM extensions enabled (table, autolink, strikethrough, tasklist, tagfilter). For progressive rendering, the parser should be able to feed content incrementally via cmark_parser_feed() and produce partial ASTs. Create a Swift-friendly wrapper around the C API that handles memory management (cmark_node_free) properly.

acceptance_criteria.description: Parses markdown using cmark-gfm with all GFM extensions enabled
Swift wrapper handles cmark memory management safely (no leaks)
Supports incremental feeding via cmark_parser_feed() for progressive parsing
Can parse a 1MB file in under 15ms on background thread

architecture.memory: Swift class wraps cmark_node pointer, frees in deinit
architecture.incremental: Feed 10KB chunks, produce partial AST after each chunk

steps.title: Implement AST-to-AttributedString styling stage
steps.description: Implement the MarkdownStyler pipeline stage. Walks the cmark AST and produces NSAttributedString with appropriate attributes for each node type. Headings get sized fonts and weights. Code blocks get monospace font and background color. Bold/italic get appropriate traits. Links get link attributes. This stage consumes theme colors/fonts (via a ThemeProvider protocol dependency, stubbed for now). Process in chunks — yield RenderChunks as blocks are styled, do not wait for the entire document.

acceptance_criteria.description: Headings (h1-h6) produce attributed strings with distinct sizes and weights
Bold, italic, strikethrough, inline code produce correct font traits
Code blocks use monospace font with distinct background attribute
Links produce attributed strings with NSAttributedString.Key.link
Lists, blockquotes, tables, horizontal rules produce styled output
Styling is chunk-based — yields RenderChunks progressively
Takes colors/fonts from a ThemeProvider protocol (stubbed with defaults)

architecture.pattern: AST visitor that yields styled chunks
architecture.dependency: ThemeProvider protocol for colors and fonts
architecture.chunking: Yield RenderChunk per top-level block element

steps.title: Integrate TreeSitter for syntax highlighting
steps.description: Add TreeSitter as a dependency and implement the SyntaxHighlighter stage. For each code block in the AST that has a language identifier, use TreeSitter to parse the code and apply syntax highlighting colors from the theme. Support the primary languages: Swift, Python, JavaScript/TypeScript, Go, Rust, Java, C/C++, Ruby, Shell, SQL, JSON, YAML, HTML, CSS. Unknown languages fall back to plain monospace. TreeSitter grammars should be bundled as compiled .dylib or compiled at build time.

acceptance_criteria.description: TreeSitter added as SPM dependency and compiles
Syntax highlighting works for Swift, Python, JS, Go, Rust, Java, C, Ruby, Shell, SQL, JSON, YAML, HTML, CSS
Unknown languages render as plain monospace without errors
Highlighting colors sourced from ThemeProvider protocol
Per-block highlighting completes in under 5ms for typical code blocks

architecture.grammars: Bundled, compiled at build time for each supported language
architecture.performance: Incremental — only re-highlight visible code blocks on theme change

steps.title: Wire pipeline stages to NSTextContentStorage for progressive display
steps.description: Connect the pipeline output (RenderChunks of NSAttributedString) to NSTextContentStorage for display in the NSTextView. Implement progressive rendering: as each chunk is styled, append it to the text content storage on the main thread. The first screenful of content should appear before the full document is processed. Use NSTextContentStorage.performEditingTransaction() for efficient batch updates. Ensure TextKit 2's lazy layout only computes layout for the visible viewport.

acceptance_criteria.description: Styled chunks append to NSTextContentStorage as they are produced
First screenful visible before full document is parsed for files > 50KB
Uses performEditingTransaction() for batch updates
TextKit 2 lazy layout active — only visible viewport laid out
Main thread receives styled output, never does parsing or styling

architecture.delivery: Background stages produce RenderChunks -> MainActor receives and appends to NSTextContentStorage
architecture.progressive: First ~10KB styled and displayed, then remaining content streams in

steps.title: Implement pipeline cancellation and re-render support
steps.description: Add cancellation support to the pipeline for when a new file is opened in the same window (if ever needed) or when a theme changes and the document needs re-rendering. Use Swift structured concurrency (TaskGroup) so cancelling the parent task cascades to all pipeline stages. Implement re-render: when theme changes, cancel current pipeline, clear text storage, restart with same document but new theme provider. Ensure no race conditions between cancellation and main-thread delivery.

acceptance_criteria.description: Cancelling a pipeline run stops all stages and releases resources
Theme change triggers full re-render with new colors/fonts
No race conditions between cancellation and NSTextContentStorage updates
Re-render for a typical file completes in under 200ms

architecture.cancellation: Swift Task cancellation via structured concurrency
architecture.safety: Check Task.isCancelled before each main-thread dispatch
architecture.cleanup: Clear NSTextContentStorage before appending re-rendered content
